content.full_text:
Suzan D. Hatfield
ATTORNEY AI LAW
220 NELLEN AVENI'E
CORTE MADERA, CA 9'925
1415)9244tfr
Frs I
February 13, 1990
John Rothman
Legal Counsel , Legal Divis'ion
Fa'ir Pol itical Practices Commission
P. 0. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Dear Mr. Rothman:
This letter follows our telephone conversation on February 9 in which we
discussed the disclosure requirencnts appl icable to the District's elected
off ic'ials and employees under the Fair Political Practices Act. You have
advised me that those elected officials deemed to be "other public off ic'ials
who manage publ ic investments" are required to file Forn 721 as well as Form
730 because they are deemed to have assumed office effective January I of
1990. After this year, those officjals wjll be required to fjle a Form 730
annually by April I covering the preceding year. In that regard, Distnict
board members and employees deemed to be public officials managing public
investments are, as Article II officials, not subiect to the Distrjct's ov{n
Confl ict of Interest Code, but to the state's reporting requirements only.
l,le discussed filing ob1 igat'ions of the rest of the District's designated
employees and you advised me that the FPPC has taken the position in the
41 perin matter (3 FPPC 79) that local agencies may not require local
offi ci al s and empl oyees, pursuant to a I ocal confl j ct of interest code, to
meet more strjngent conflicts requirements than is required of Article II
employees. In that regard, therefore, designated employees of a 1oca1 agency
other than those who are "pub1 ic officials managing public investments" would
be deemed to be in compliance wjth Artjcle III if the local agency were to
require them to annually file Form 730 so long as the local code did not
contain provis'ions which were more stringent than state law.
Please let me know if my view of the conclus'ions reached in our conversation
var'ies from your un<ierstanding of the law jn 
this 
area.
I appreciate your assistance in this matter.
S i ncerel
Hatfi el d
SDH ; eb

sections.question_synthetic: The letter confirms FPPC guidance regarding disclosure filing requirements for elected officials and employees under the Fair Political Practices Act, specifically regarding Form 721 and Form 730 filing obligations and the applicability of local conflict of interest codes to different classes of public officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter from an attorney (Suzan D. Hatfield) seeking confirmation of FPPC guidance rather than an FPPC response letter. The document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections typical of FPPC advice letters. The letter references prior FPPC guidance on telephone and cites the Alperin case (3 FPPC 79). Document quality shows OCR artifacts (e.g., 'NELLEN AVENI'E', 'Fa'ir', 'off ic'ials').

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The letter confirms FPPC guidance regarding disclosure filing requirements for elected officials and employees under the Fair Political Practices Act, specifically regarding Form 721 and Form 730 filing obligations and the applicability of local conflict of interest codes to different classes of public officials.

Hatfield ATTORNEY AI LAW 220 NELLEN AVENI'E CORTE MADERA, CA 9'925 1415)9244tfr Frs I February 13, 1990 John Rothman Legal Counsel , Legal Divis'ion Fa'ir Pol itical Practices Commission P. 0. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Dear Mr. Rothman: This letter follows our telephone conversation on February 9 in which we discussed the disclosure requirencnts appl icable to the District's elected off ic'ials and employees under the Fair Political Practices Act. You have advised me that those elected officials deemed to be "other public off ic'ials who manage publ ic investments" are required to file Forn 721 as well as Form 730 because they are deemed to have assumed office effective January I of 1990. After this year, those officjals wjll be required to fjle a Form 730 annually by April I covering the preceding year. In that regard, Distnict board members and employees deemed to be public officials managing public investments are, as Article II officials, not subiect to the Distrjct's ov{n Confl ict of Interest Code, but to the state's reporting requirements only. l,le discussed filing ob1 igat'ions of the rest of the District's designated employees and you advised me that the FPPC has taken the position in the 41 perin matter (3 FPPC 79) that local agencies may not require local offi ci al s and empl oyees, pursuant to a I ocal confl j ct of interest code, to meet more strjngent conflicts requirements than is required of Article II employees. In that regard, therefore, designated employees of a 1oca1 agency other than those who are "pub1 ic officials managing public investments" would be deemed to be in compliance wjth Artjcle III if the local agency were to require them to annually file Form 730 so long as the local code did not contain provis'ions which were more stringent than state law. Please let me know if my view of the conclus'ions reached in our conversation var'ies from your un<ierstanding of the law jn this area. I appreciate your assistance in this matter. S i ncerel Hatfi el d SDH ; eb
embedding.summary: This is a follow-up letter from an attorney to FPPC Legal Counsel summarizing guidance received during a telephone conversation regarding disclosure filing requirements for district officials and employees, particularly concerning Form 721/730 filings and the limitations on local conflict of interest codes under the Alperin precedent.
